title: Cleanup code to more closely match Docker Build images
body:
Hostname should be set to a random name if not set.
Workdir should default to "" not to "/"
If Volumes and Entrypoint are not set they should return NULL not []

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Changes LGTM, but I want to run it through a baseline test.  I think the entrypoint change is OK, but want to verify.

The majority of the tests ran fine, but the test that pushes a bulidah image to Docker and then runs the image using Docker is failing.  

```
FILE=./runecho.sh
/bin/cat <<EOM >$FILE
#!/bin/bash
for i in {1..9};
do
    echo "This is a new container from ipbabble [" \$i "]"
done
EOM
chmod +x $FILE

# newcontainer=$(buildah from scratch)
# scratchmnt=$(buildah mount $newcontainer)
# dnf install --installroot $scratchmnt --release 28 bash coreutils --setopt install_weak_deps=false -y
# buildah copy $newcontainer $FILE /usr/bin
# buildah config --cmd /usr/bin/runecho.sh $newcontainer
# buildah run $newcontainer /usr/bin/runecho.sh 
# buildah unmount $newcontainer
# buildah commit $newcontainer fedora-bashecho
# buildah push fedora-bashecho docker-daemon:fedora-bashecho:latest

# docker run fedora-bashecho
standard_init_linux.go:178: exec user process caused "permission denied"

```
I'm backing out Buildah, installing latest rpm to see if it's a vm config issue...
Hmm, same error with the Buildah released kit on board, not this change, but still worrisome.  More digging.
FWIW, something was funky in the installed docker dirs.  I removed /var/lib/docker, restarted docker and then it worked just fine.
